label: Security-first guidance
description: Applicable to all LLMs for secure engineering practices.
value:
## Security-first Guidance

- Validate all external input at boundaries. Never trust data from users, APIs, or files.
- Never hardcode credentials, API keys, or secrets.
- Prefer safe defaults and explicit permission checks.
- Escalate unknown or ambiguous security issues instead of guessing.

label: Small focused changes
description: Use small, incremental updates across all AI configurations.
value:
## Small Focused Changes

- Keep each change narrow and self-contained.
- Avoid large refactors unless explicitly requested.
- Prefer incremental improvements that are easier to review and test.

label: Shared language conventions
description: Apply shared coding conventions across different language-specific configs.
value:
## Shared Language Conventions

- Follow the existing project conventions for naming, style, and architecture.
- Prefer consistency with adjacent code rather than introducing new idioms.
- Document deviations clearly in comments or README when they are necessary.
